checks.a_analysis: I have compared the output JSON against the source PDF. All textual content in the JSON, including headings, agenda items, and the meeting schedule, is present in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. The table caption and image alt text are acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility.

The only contact-related information in the document is the URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc'. This URL in the JSON output exactly matches the URL provided at the bottom of the source document. No phone numbers or email addresses were present in the source or fabricated in the output.

The source document is a single-page agenda. The output JSON captures all sections of this page, including the header, the date/time/location info, the main agenda table, the upcoming meeting dates, and the website footer.

The source document contains one table with 6 agenda items (plus header). The JSON output correctly reproduces this table with all 6 data rows and 1 header row. In row 5, the sub-bullets and corresponding list of names are correctly grouped within their respective cells, preserving the source's informational structure.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All text content from the source PDF is present in the JSON. This includes the full list of committee reports and the presenters associated with them in item 5, as well as the 'DISCUSSION AND COUNCIL VOTE' note in item 4.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the JSON matches the source document's wording precisely. Minor differences in whitespace or character representation from OCR are negligible and do not alter the meaning. Punctuation and dates (May 6, 2020; February 12, 2020; August 12; November 4) are all correct.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed both the source PDF and the JSON output. All content from the source appears exactly once in the output. There are no duplicated paragraphs, repeated sections, or content appearing multiple times in different formats.

The output uses Heading Level 3 (H3) for all major sections: the council name, the word 'AGENDA', and the meeting dates footer. While it starts at H3 (skipping H1 and H2), the internal hierarchy is consistent and flat, which is permitted as a normalization adjustment for accessibility according to the task instructions.

The table structure in the output matches the source. It correctly identifies the 3-column header row and the 6 subsequent data rows. Column grouping and row alignment are preserved, even for complex rows like item 5 where multiple items and names are listed.

The reading order in the JSON output follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the original document, starting with the header/logo, followed by the agenda title, details, the main table, and ending with the meeting dates and website link.

Agenda item 5 in the table contains a bulleted list of committee reports. In the output, these 6 distinct list items are merged into a single text block within a table cell using newline characters and bullet symbols, rather than being structural list elements. This constitutes a merging of list items.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• Statewide Mapping Advisory
• Local Government
• Federal Interagency
• Technical Advisory
• State Government GIS Users
• Management & Operations
checks.b_evidence_output:

• Statewide Mapping Advisory
• Local Government
• Federal Interagency
• Technical Advisory
• State Government GIS Users
• Management & Operations"

checks.a_analysis: The website URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' provided at the bottom of the document is correctly captured as a link in the output JSON. No additional links were injected and none were missed.

checks.a_analysis: The overall layout of the HTML follows the source PDF. The title, logo, agenda header, and table are all present in the expected order. While there are minor styling differences (like the meeting dates being horizontal instead of vertical), the visual organization of information is preserved. The addition of a Table of Contents and 'Main Agenda' heading are expected accessible design features.

The main agenda table is rendered with borders and distinct headers. However, in item 5 (Committee Reports), the HTML fails to maintain the visual alignment between the committee names and the responsible individuals. In the source PDF, these are clearly paired and aligned. In the HTML output, the committee names are lumped into one cell and the individuals' names into another, making it impossible for a sighted user to determine which person belongs to which committee without prior knowledge or careful counting. This constitutes a visual corruption of the data mapping within the table.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the table row for item 5 (Committee Reports), the committees (e.g., 'Statewide Mapping Advisory') and their members (e.g., 'Paul Badr') are listed in two columns and are horizontally aligned to indicate a one-to-one relationship.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML table row 5, the agenda items '• Statewide Mapping Advisory • Local Government...' and the names 'Paul Badr Debbie Brannan...' are rendered as continuous blocks of text in their respective cells, losing the horizontal alignment and the visual relationship between the items.

checks.a_analysis: The NCGICC logo present at the top of the source PDF is correctly rendered and visible in the HTML output under the 'Main Agenda' section.
There is no evidence of duplicated body content in the HTML output. Titles and headings appear in the TOC and the body, which is an expected and acceptable accessibility feature.
